Wraps `query_create_account_with_actor` with a default `password_hash`
so suites that don't exercise password verification can stay terse.
Replaces the per-file `create_user` / `create_test_actor` /
`create_test_account` helpers that had accumulated across the auth
test suite.
declarations.type_signature: (db: Db, options: { username: string; password_hash?: string | undefined; }): Promise<TestAccountWithActor>

modules.module_comment:
DB-backed entity factories for tests that need real `account` + `actor`
rows in the database.

Companion to `entities.ts` — that file ships in-memory factories
(`create_test_account`, `create_test_actor`) for tests that mock the
DB; this file ships factories that hit a real `Db` so query-level
tests don't reimplement the same `query_create_account_with_actor`
wrapper in every file.

For full-fledged test accounts that also need an API token + signed
session cookie + permits, use `bootstrap_test_account` from
`app_server.ts` instead.

declarations.name: IS_CI
declarations.doc_comment: CI detection — `CI=true` is set automatically by GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, etc.

declarations.name: DbFactory
declarations.doc_comment: Factory interface for creating test database instances.

declarations.name: reset_pglite
declarations.doc_comment:
Reset a PGlite database to a clean state by dropping and recreating the public schema.

Removes all tables, sequences, indexes, types, and functions.
The database instance remains usable after reset.

declarations.name: create_pglite_factory
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a pglite (in-memory) database factory for tests.

Always enabled — no external dependencies required.
Shares a single PGlite WASM instance across all factories in the same
vitest worker thread (one test file). Subsequent `create()` calls reset
the schema via `DROP SCHEMA public CASCADE` instead of paying the WASM
cold-start cost again.

parameters.name: init_schema
parameters.description: callback to initialize the database schema

declarations.name: create_pg_factory
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a pg (PostgreSQL) database factory for tests.

Skipped when `test_url` is not provided.
Drops `schema_version` before running `init_schema`, forcing migrations
to re-evaluate against the actual tables. Prevents stale tracker rows
from skipping migrations when DDL changes between test sessions.

For full clean-slate behavior (recommended), call `drop_auth_schema(db)`
at the start of `init_schema` before running migrations. This handles
upstream schema changes that go beyond adding new tables/columns.
declarations.type_signature: (init_schema: (db: Db) => Promise<void>, test_url?: string | undefined): DbFactory
declarations.return_description:
a factory that creates pg databases. The returned `create()`
throws when `test_url` is unset (despite the `skip: true` flag — defense
against direct invocation), and rewrites Postgres "database does not
exist" errors into a `createdb` hint message.

parameters.name: init_schema
parameters.description: callback to initialize the database schema

declarations.name: AUTH_TRUNCATE_TABLES
declarations.doc_comment:
Auth table names in truncation order (children first for FK safety).

Consumer projects can spread this into their own list and append app-specific tables.

declarations.name: AUTH_INTEGRATION_TRUNCATE_TABLES
declarations.doc_comment:
Auth tables including `audit_log` — for integration tests that exercise
the full middleware stack (login, admin, rate limiting).

Separate from `AUTH_TRUNCATE_TABLES` because unit-level DB tests that don't
touch audit logging don't need to truncate it.

declarations.name: AUTH_DROP_TABLES
declarations.doc_comment:
All auth tables in drop order (children first for FK safety).

The full set created by `AUTH_MIGRATIONS` — use for clean-slate
test DB initialization. `AUTH_TRUNCATE_TABLES` is the subset for
between-test data cleanup (excludes `audit_log`).

When adding tables to `AUTH_MIGRATIONS`, add them here too.

declarations.name: drop_auth_schema
declarations.doc_comment:
Drop all auth tables and schema version tracking for a clean slate.

Recommended at the start of `init_schema` callbacks for `create_pg_factory`.
Persistent test databases can accumulate stale schema from previous fuz_app
versions — this ensures migrations run against a truly empty database.
Safe on fresh databases (`IF EXISTS` on all statements). No-op effect for
PGlite (already fresh), but harmless to call unconditionally.

declarations.name: create_describe_db
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a `describe_db` function bound to specific factories and truncate tables.

Returns a 2-arg `(name, fn)` function that runs the test suite against each
factory. Each factory gets its own `describe` block with a shared database
instance, automatic `beforeEach` truncation, and `afterAll` cleanup.
Skipped factories use `describe.skip`.
declarations.type_signature: (factories: DbFactory | DbFactory[], truncate_tables: string[]): (name: string, fn: (get_db: () => Db) => void) => void

parameters.name: factories
parameters.description: one or more database factories to run suites against

parameters.name: truncate_tables
parameters.description: tables to truncate between tests (children first for FK safety)

declarations.name: log_db_factory_status
declarations.doc_comment: Log factory status to console.

declarations.name: TestAccountOverrides
declarations.doc_comment: Override type for `create_test_account` — id-like fields accept plain `string`.

declarations.name: create_test_account
declarations.doc_comment: Create a test `Account` with sensible defaults.

declarations.name: TestActorOverrides
declarations.doc_comment: Override type for `create_test_actor` — id-like fields accept plain `string`.

declarations.name: create_test_actor
declarations.doc_comment: Create a test `Actor` with sensible defaults.

declarations.name: TestPermitOverrides
declarations.doc_comment: Override type for `create_test_permit` — id-like fields accept plain `string`.

declarations.name: create_test_permit
declarations.doc_comment: Create a test `Permit` with sensible defaults.

declarations.name: create_test_context
declarations.doc_comment: Create a test `RequestContext` with permits from partial overrides.

declarations.name: TestAuditEventOverrides
declarations.doc_comment: Override type for `create_test_audit_event` — id-like fields accept plain `string`.

declarations.name: create_test_audit_event
declarations.doc_comment: Create a test `AuditLogEvent` with sensible defaults.

modules.module_comment:
Shared test entity factories for `Account`, `Actor`, `Permit`, `AuditLogEvent`,
and `RequestContext`.

Override types widen branded `Uuid` fields to `string` so tests can pass
literal ids without per-call-site casts. The factory brands internally.

Uses `create_test_*` names to avoid collisions with real
`create_account_with_actor` from `auth/account_queries.ts`.

declarations.name: extract_declared_error_codes
declarations.doc_comment:
Extract declared error code values from an error response schema.

Recognizes schemas shaped like `z.object({error: z.literal(...)})` or
`z.object({error: z.enum([...])})` (incl. `looseObject`/`strictObject`).
Returns the set of declared code values, or `null` if the schema doesn't
expose a literal/enum `error` field (e.g., bare `ApiError` with `z.string()`).

Used by coverage reporting to split a single declared status into per-code
rows when the route's error schema names specific codes.

declarations.name: UncoveredEntry
declarations.doc_comment: Uncovered entry — either a status-level row (no `code`) or a specific-code row.

properties.name: code
properties.doc_comment: Declared code value missing, when the status's error schema names specific codes.

declarations.name: CoverageFilterOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options controlling which routes/statuses are considered for coverage.

properties.name: ignore_routes
properties.doc_comment: Routes to skip, in `'METHOD /path'` format.

properties.name: ignore_statuses
properties.doc_comment: HTTP status codes to skip.

declarations.name: ErrorCoverageCollector
declarations.doc_comment:
Tracks which route × status (and route × status × code) combinations have
been exercised in tests.

Use `record()` to log an observed status (optionally with the body's `error`
code), or `assert_and_record()` to combine response validation with tracking
(auto-extracts `body.error` from the response when present).
After all tests, call `uncovered()` to find declared error paths never
exercised.

An observation recorded without a code still satisfies "any-code" coverage
requirements for the same status — i.e., if a caller records just the status,
all declared codes for that status are considered covered. Per-code tracking
is additive: callers who know the body's `error` value should pass it to get
precise per-code gap reporting on routes with literal/enum error schemas.

members.name: observed
members.doc_comment:
Observed keys: `"METHOD /spec-path:STATUS"` or `"METHOD /spec-path:STATUS:CODE"`.

Both shapes coexist — the code-less key marks the status as covered at any
code; a code-bearing key adds per-code precision.

members.name: record
members.doc_comment:
Record an observed error status (optionally with the body's `error` code) for a route.

Resolves the concrete request path back to the spec template path
(e.g., `/api/accounts/abc` → `/api/accounts/:id`). When `code` is provided,
it is stored alongside the status for per-code coverage tracking.
members.type_signature: (route_specs: RouteSpec[], method: string, path: string, status: number, code?: string | undefined): void

parameters.name: code
parameters.description:
observed body `error` code (pass when the route's error
schema declares specific codes via `z.literal` or `z.enum`)

members.name: assert_and_record
members.doc_comment:
Validate a response against its route spec and record the status.

Wraps `assert_response_matches_spec` and records the status code. For
error responses, auto-extracts `body.error` from the JSON body (via a
cloned response, so the original stream stays usable) and records it
for per-code coverage. Pass an explicit `code` to override the
auto-extracted value or when the body was already consumed.
members.type_signature: (route_specs: RouteSpec[], method: string, path: string, response: Response, code?: string | undefined): Promise<void>

parameters.name: code
parameters.description:
observed body `error` code (override; if omitted and the
response body is a JSON object with a string `error` field, that value
is auto-extracted)

throws.description: if the response body fails the route spec's declared

members.name: uncovered
members.doc_comment:
Find declared error paths that were never observed.

Computes the declared set from `merge_error_schemas` for each route spec.
For statuses whose error schema names specific codes (via `z.literal` or
`z.enum`), reports per-code rows; otherwise reports one row per status.
A status-only observation (no code) satisfies all declared codes for that
status — the "any-code" rule.

declarations.name: DEFAULT_INTEGRATION_ERROR_COVERAGE
declarations.doc_comment:
Default minimum error coverage threshold for the standard integration
and admin test suites. Conservative — not all error paths are exercisable
in the composable suites. Consumers should increase as their test suites mature.

declarations.name: ErrorCoverageOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `assert_error_coverage`.

properties.name: min_coverage
properties.doc_comment: Minimum coverage ratio (0–1). Default `0` (informational only).

declarations.name: assert_error_coverage
declarations.doc_comment:
Assert error coverage meets a minimum threshold.

Computes the ratio of exercised error paths to total declared error paths.
For routes whose status error schema names specific codes (`z.literal` or
`z.enum`), each declared code counts as one coverage path; for schemas
without declared codes (`ApiError`/`z.string()`), the status counts as one
path. A status-only observation covers all declared codes for that status
(the "any-code" rule).

When `min_coverage` is 0 (default), logs coverage info without failing.
When > 0, fails if coverage is below the threshold.

throws.description: if `min_coverage > 0` and the covered/total ratio

declarations.type_signature: (collector: ErrorCoverageCollector, route_specs: RouteSpec[], options?: ErrorCoverageOptions | undefined): void

modules.module_comment:
Error reachability coverage tracking.

Tracks which declared error statuses (and specific error codes) are
actually exercised in tests. `ErrorCoverageCollector` records status
codes (optionally with body `error` codes) observed during test runs,
then `assert_error_coverage` compares against declared error schemas
to find uncovered error paths — reporting per-code when the declared
schema is a literal or enum, per-status otherwise.

declarations.name: find_route_spec
declarations.doc_comment:
Find a route spec matching the given method and path.

Supports both exact matches and parameterized paths (`:param` segments).
declarations.type_signature: (specs: RouteSpec[], method: string, path: string): RouteSpec | undefined

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: request path (exact or with concrete param values)

declarations.name: REST_AUTH_ROUTE_SUFFIXES
declarations.doc_comment:
REST auth route suffixes still on the account/bootstrap surface after the
2026-04-22 RPC migration. `find_auth_route` rejects any other suffix at
runtime — session/token CRUD, admin operations, and permit flows live on
the RPC surface and should be reached via `rpc_call`.

declarations.name: find_auth_route
declarations.doc_comment:
Find a REST auth route by suffix and method.

Decouples tests from consumer route prefix (`/api/account/login`,
`/api/auth/login`, etc.). `suffix` must be one of
`REST_AUTH_ROUTE_SUFFIXES` — throws otherwise so a post-migration RPC
method name (e.g. `/sessions/revoke-all`) fails loudly at the call site
instead of silently returning `undefined`.

throws.description: if `suffix` is not in `REST_AUTH_ROUTE_SUFFIXES`.

declarations.type_signature: (specs: RouteSpec[], suffix: "/login" | "/logout" | "/password" | "/verify" | "/signup" | "/bootstrap", method: RouteMethod): RouteSpec | undefined

declarations.name: assert_response_matches_spec
declarations.doc_comment:
Validate a response body against the route spec's declared schemas.

For 2xx responses, validates against `spec.output`.
For error responses, validates against the merged error schema for that status code.

throws.description: if no route spec matches `method` + `path`, if the response

declarations.type_signature: (route_specs: RouteSpec[], method: string, path: string, response: Response): Promise<void>

declarations.name: create_expired_test_cookie
declarations.doc_comment: Create an expired test cookie — validly signed but with an expiry timestamp in 1970.

declarations.name: check_error_response_fields
declarations.doc_comment:
List the fields in an error response body that are not in the known-safe set.

Error schemas use `z.looseObject` (intentional — multiple producers), but
test responses should be checked for fields that could leak information.
declarations.return_description: array of unexpected field names (empty = clean)

declarations.name: assert_no_error_info_leakage
declarations.doc_comment:
Assert that an error response contains no leaky field values.

Checks both field names and string values for patterns indicating
stack traces, SQL, or internal paths. Accepts `unknown` so callers
pass response bodies / nested envelope fields directly without
intermediate `as` casts; non-object bodies skip the field-name check.

declarations.name: assert_rate_limit_retry_after_header
declarations.doc_comment:
Assert that a 429 response includes a valid `Retry-After` header
matching the JSON body's `retry_after` field.

declarations.name: SENSITIVE_FIELD_BLOCKLIST
declarations.doc_comment: Field names that must never appear in any HTTP response body.

declarations.name: ADMIN_ONLY_FIELD_BLOCKLIST
declarations.doc_comment: Field names that must not appear in non-admin HTTP response bodies.

declarations.name: collect_json_keys_recursive
declarations.doc_comment:
Recursively collect all key names from a parsed JSON value.

Walks objects and arrays to find every property name at any nesting depth.

declarations.name: assert_no_sensitive_fields_in_json
declarations.doc_comment: Assert that a parsed JSON body contains no fields from the given blocklist.
declarations.type_signature: (body: unknown, blocklist: readonly string[], context: string): void

declarations.name: pick_auth_headers
declarations.doc_comment:
Pick request headers matching a route spec's auth requirement.

Maps `RouteAuth` onto a test account's credentials:
- `none` — origin headers only
- `authenticated` — the authed account's session cookie
- `role: admin` — the admin account's session cookie
- `role: <other>` — the test app's bootstrapped keeper session
- `keeper` — the test app's daemon token
declarations.type_signature: (spec: RouteSpec, test_app: TestApp, authed_account: TestAccount, admin_account: TestAccount): Record<string, string>

modules.module_comment: Integration test helpers — route lookup, response validation, and cookie utilities.

declarations.name: StandardIntegrationTestOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Configuration for `describe_standard_integration_tests`.

properties.name: session_options
properties.doc_comment: Session config for cookie-based auth.

properties.name: create_route_specs
properties.doc_comment: Route spec factory — same one used in production.

properties.name: app_options
properties.doc_comment: Optional overrides for `AppServerOptions`.

properties.name: db_factories
properties.doc_comment:
Database factories to run tests against. Default: pglite only.
Pass consumer factories (e.g. `[pglite_factory, pg_factory]`) to also test against PostgreSQL.

properties.name: rpc_endpoints
properties.doc_comment:
RPC endpoint specs — required. This suite dispatches
`account_verify`, `account_session_*`, and `account_token_*` via
`rpc_call_for_spec` (the `/api/account/verify` REST route is a
status-only nginx shim with no payload). Hard-fails via
`require_rpc_endpoint_path` on setup so consumer projects see a
clear setup error instead of confusing test failures.

Accepts either an array (eager) or a factory
`(ctx: AppServerContext) => Array<RpcEndpointSpec>` — the factory form
is required when action handlers must close over the per-test
`ctx.app_settings` / `ctx.deps` (e.g. the canonical
`create_standard_rpc_actions(ctx.deps, {app_settings: ctx.app_settings})`
pattern). The factory must return the same endpoint `path` regardless
of ctx — it is invoked once at setup with a stub ctx for path lookup
and again per-test by `create_app_server` for live dispatch.

declarations.name: describe_standard_integration_tests
declarations.doc_comment:
Standard integration test suite for fuz_app auth routes.

Exercises login/logout, cookie attributes, session security, session
revocation, password change (incl. API token revocation), origin
verification, bearer auth (incl. browser context discard on mutations),
token revocation, cross-account isolation, expired credential rejection,
signup invite edge cases, and response body validation.

Each test group asserts that required routes exist, failing with a descriptive
message if the consumer's route specs are misconfigured.

throws.description: at setup time when `options.rpc_endpoints` is empty — the

modules.module_comment:
Standard integration test suite for fuz_app auth routes.

`describe_standard_integration_tests` creates a composable test suite that
exercises the full middleware stack (origin, session, bearer_auth, request_context)
against a real PGlite database. Consumers call it with their route factory and
session config — all auth route tests come for free.

Tests use `stub_password_deps` (deterministic hashing, no Argon2 overhead).
Login handlers call `verify_password(submitted, stored_hash)` which works because
both hash and verify use the same stub logic.

Rate limiters are disabled by default — tests make many login attempts and would
trigger limits otherwise.

declarations.name: BearerAuthTestOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Mock configuration for bearer auth middleware test setup.

properties.name: headers

properties.name: pre_context

Create mock dependencies for `create_bearer_auth_middleware`, configured per test case.

Configures the module-level mocks for `query_validate_api_token`,

so each test case controls return values independently.

Create a Hono app wired with `create_bearer_auth_middleware` using mocked deps.

The route handler at `/api/test` returns the resolved context in the response body,
enabling assertions on `REQUEST_CONTEXT_KEY` and `CREDENTIAL_TYPE_KEY`.

Run a table of bearer auth middleware test cases.

Create a Hono app with real proxy + origin + bearer middleware for integration testing.

All DB queries return undefined (no real database needed).

modules.module_comment:
Table-driven middleware test helpers.

Provides mock builders for bearer auth middleware dependencies,
a generic test runner that iterates case tables, and a reusable
middleware stack factory for integration testing.

declarations.name: MockFs

In-memory file system mock for tests that use dependency-injected

Creates an in-memory file system for tests.

`read_file` throws an `ENOENT`-tagged error for missing paths so callers
can exercise the same "file doesn't exist" code path as `node:fs`.

modules.module_comment:
In-memory file system mock for tests that use dependency-injected
`read_file` and `write_file` callbacks. Avoids module-level mocking.

declarations.name: RateLimitingTestOptions

Maximum attempts before rate limiting kicks in.

properties.name: rpc_endpoints

RPC endpoint specs — required so the bearer-auth rate limiting test
can probe an authenticated method via the `account_verify` RPC
action. Hard-fails via `require_rpc_endpoint_path` on setup.

Accepts either an array (eager) or a factory
`(ctx: AppServerContext) => Array<RpcEndpointSpec>` — the factory form
is required when action handlers must close over the per-test
`ctx.app_settings` / `ctx.deps`. The factory must return the same
endpoint `path` regardless of ctx — it is invoked once at setup with
a stub ctx for path lookup and again per-test by `create_app_server`
for live dispatch.

declarations.name: describe_rate_limiting_tests
declarations.doc_comment:
Standard rate limiting integration test suite.

Creates 3 test groups:
1. IP rate limiting on login — fires `max_attempts + 1` login requests,
   verifies the last returns 429 with a valid `RateLimitError` body.
2. Per-account rate limiting on login — fires `max_attempts + 1` login
   requests with the same username, verifies the last returns 429.
3. Bearer auth IP rate limiting — fires `max_attempts + 1` bearer requests
   with an invalid token, verifies the last returns 429.

Each test group asserts that required routes exist, failing with a descriptive
message if the consumer's route specs are misconfigured.

throws.description: at setup time when `options.rpc_endpoints` is empty — the

modules.module_comment:
Rate limiting integration test suite.

Verifies that sensitive routes (login, bootstrap, token creation) enforce
rate limits when rate limiters are enabled. Tests create a tight rate limiter
(2 attempts / 1 minute) and fire requests until 429 is returned.

Consumers call `describe_rate_limiting_tests` with their route factory and
session config — rate limit enforcement tests come for free.

declarations.name: RoundTripTestOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `describe_round_trip_validation`.

properties.name: session_options
properties.doc_comment: Session config for cookie-based auth.

properties.name: create_route_specs
properties.doc_comment: Route spec factory — same one used in production.

properties.name: app_options
properties.doc_comment: Optional overrides for `AppServerOptions`.

properties.name: db_factories
properties.doc_comment: Database factories to run tests against. Default: pglite only.

properties.name: skip_routes
properties.doc_comment: Routes to skip, in `'METHOD /path'` format.

properties.name: input_overrides
properties.doc_comment: Override generated bodies for specific routes (`'METHOD /path'` → body).

declarations.name: describe_round_trip_validation
declarations.doc_comment:
Run schema-driven round-trip validation tests.

For each route:
1. Resolve URL with valid params
2. Generate a valid request body (or use override)
3. Pick auth headers matching the route's auth requirement
4. Fire the request and validate the response against declared schemas

SSE routes are skipped (Content-Type `text/event-stream`).
Routes returning non-2xx with valid input are still validated against
their declared error schemas.

modules.module_comment:
Schema-driven round-trip validation test suite.

For every route spec, generates a valid request (auth, params, body)
and validates the response against declared output or error schemas.
DB-backed via `create_test_app` — exercises the full middleware stack.

declarations.name: RpcAttackSurfaceOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `describe_rpc_attack_surface_tests`.

properties.name: build
properties.doc_comment: Build the app surface bundle (surface + route specs + middleware specs + rpc_endpoints).

properties.name: roles
properties.doc_comment: All roles in the app (e.g. `['admin', 'keeper']`).

declarations.name: describe_rpc_attack_surface_tests
declarations.doc_comment:
Run the standard RPC attack surface test suite.

Generates 3 test groups:
1. Auth enforcement — per-method auth checks via JSON-RPC envelopes
2. Adversarial envelopes — malformed JSON-RPC requests
3. Adversarial params — schema-invalid params per method

Skips silently when `surface.rpc_endpoints` is empty.

modules.module_comment:
Composable RPC attack surface test suite.

Three test groups for JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoints:
1. **Auth enforcement** — per-method auth inside the dispatcher
2. **Adversarial envelopes** — malformed JSON-RPC requests
3. **Adversarial params** — schema-invalid params per method

Uses the same `{build, roles}` config as `describe_adversarial_auth`
and `describe_adversarial_input`. No DB needed — uses stub deps.

declarations.name: RpcEndpointsSuiteOption
declarations.doc_comment:
Union accepted by the suite-level `rpc_endpoints` option — eager array or
a factory that takes an `AppServerContext` and returns endpoint specs. The
factory form is required when action handlers must close over the
per-test `ctx.app_settings` / `ctx.deps` (e.g. the canonical
`create_standard_rpc_actions(ctx.deps, {app_settings: ctx.app_settings})`
pattern). `create_app_server` resolves either shape natively; test helpers
forward the raw value to `app_options.rpc_endpoints` for live dispatch.

declarations.name: resolve_rpc_endpoints_for_setup
declarations.doc_comment:
Resolve a suite's `rpc_endpoints` option to an array for setup-time
inspection (path lookup, action presence checks).

For the factory form this invokes the factory twice with stub
`AppServerContext`s and asserts that both invocations produce the same
(path, method-list) shape — catching factories that close over mutable
state or otherwise diverge across calls. The first array is returned;
the second is discarded after the comparison. `create_app_server`
invokes the factory again per-test with its real ctx, and those are
the handlers that actually serve requests.

Safe as long as the factory is pure with respect to the endpoint `path`
and the action `spec.method` list — the canonical helpers
(`create_standard_rpc_actions`, `create_admin_actions`, `create_account_actions`,
etc.) are. Factories that return a different `path` based on `ctx` will
produce a setup/runtime mismatch; the path-purity assert below surfaces
that as a clear `gro check` error rather than a silent test/runtime drift.

throws.description: if the factory's two stub-ctx invocations produce different

declarations.name: create_rpc_post_init
declarations.doc_comment: Create a `RequestInit` for a JSON-RPC POST request.
declarations.type_signature: (method: string, params?: unknown, id?: string | number): RequestInit

parameters.name: params
parameters.description:
params (omit for parameterless methods; `null` is also
 stripped for ergonomic call sites — JSON-RPC 2.0 §4.2
 forbids `"params": null` on the wire, and `create_rpc_endpoint`
 rejects `z.null()` action input schemas at registration).
 Tests that need to construct a literal `"params": null`
 envelope (e.g. asserting envelope-level rejection) should
 build the body inline rather than route through this helper.

declarations.name: create_rpc_get_url
declarations.doc_comment: Build a GET URL with JSON-RPC query parameters.
declarations.type_signature: (endpoint_path: string, method: string, params?: unknown, id?: string | number): string

declarations.name: assert_jsonrpc_error_response
declarations.doc_comment:
Assert that a response body is a valid JSON-RPC error response.

Validates the structure matches `JsonrpcErrorResponse` and optionally
checks the error code.

declarations.name: assert_jsonrpc_success_response
declarations.doc_comment:
Assert that a response body is a valid JSON-RPC success response.

Validates the structure matches `JsonrpcResponse`. When `output_schema`
is provided, also validates the `result` field against the declared
output schema — matching the REST round-trip's `assert_response_matches_spec`.
declarations.type_signature: (body: unknown, output_schema?: ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>> | undefined): void

parameters.name: output_schema
parameters.description: optional Zod schema to validate the `result` field against

declarations.name: RpcTestTransport
declarations.doc_comment:
Minimal transport surface — the duck type `Hono.request` already satisfies.
Extracted so test setups that want an in-process / WS / mock path can plug
a different dispatcher without changing call sites.

declarations.name: http_transport
declarations.doc_comment: Adapt a `Hono`-style app into an `RpcTestTransport`.
declarations.type_signature: (app: { request: (input: string, init: RequestInit) => Response | Promise<Response>; }): RpcTestTransport

parameters.name: app
parameters.type: { request: (input: string, init: RequestInit) => Response | Promise<Response>; }

declarations.name: RpcCallResult
declarations.doc_comment: Discriminated return from `rpc_call`. `status` is the HTTP status.

declarations.name: RpcCallArgs
declarations.doc_comment: Arguments for `rpc_call`.

properties.name: app
properties.doc_comment: Hono-like app (anything with a `request(url, init)` method).

properties.name: path
properties.doc_comment: RPC endpoint path, e.g. `'/api/rpc'`.

properties.name: method
properties.doc_comment: JSON-RPC method name.

properties.name: params
properties.doc_comment:
Params for the call. Omit (or pass `undefined`) for parameterless
(`z.void()`) methods — the helper drops `params` from the envelope
either way. See `create_rpc_post_init` for the null-stripping
affordance and JSON-RPC 2.0 §4.2's prohibition on `params: null`.

properties.name: headers
properties.doc_comment: Extra request headers (session cookie, bearer, etc.). Overrides defaults.

properties.name: id
properties.doc_comment: Request id. Defaults to `'test'`.

properties.name: verb
properties.doc_comment: HTTP verb — `'POST'` (default) or `'GET'` for `side_effects: false` methods.

properties.name: suppress_default_origin
properties.doc_comment:
Suppress the default `origin` header. Required for bearer-auth paths:
`bearer_auth` discards the token when Origin or Referer is present
(browser context), so probing it via `rpc_call` needs this flag — or
use `rpc_call_non_browser`, which sets it for you.

declarations.name: rpc_call
declarations.doc_comment:
One-shot JSON-RPC call over a Hono app.

Merges sensible defaults (`host`, `origin`, `Content-Type`) under
caller-provided headers, fires POST (default) or GET, parses the envelope,
and returns a discriminated result.

throws.description: if the response body is neither a valid `JsonrpcResponse`

declarations.name: rpc_call_non_browser
declarations.doc_comment:
Same as `rpc_call` but without the default `origin` header. Use for
bearer-auth probes: `bearer_auth` discards the token when Origin or
Referer is present (browser context), so a bearer probe via `rpc_call`
would short-circuit to 401 before the token is ever validated.

Equivalent to `rpc_call({...args, suppress_default_origin: true})`.

declarations.name: RpcCallResultForSpec
declarations.doc_comment:
Typed discriminated result returned by `rpc_call_for_spec`. The success
branch's `result` is inferred from `TSpec['output']`. The error branch
stays untyped because JSON-RPC `error.data` shapes vary per error and
are asserted per call site.

declarations.name: RpcCallForSpecArgs
declarations.doc_comment: Arguments for `rpc_call_for_spec`. `spec` replaces the loose `method` field.

declarations.name: rpc_call_for_spec
declarations.doc_comment:
Typed wrapper over `rpc_call` — binds `params` to `z.infer<spec.input>`
and the success `result` to `z.infer<spec.output>` via the generic.

Success results are validated at runtime against `spec.output` (same
contract as `rpc_call_typed`); a mismatch throws. Error responses come
back on the discriminated `{ok: false, error}` branch — use this for
happy-path + denial-path assertions where the error `data.reason` shape
is still asserted manually. For adversarial input tests that send
malformed params, use the untyped `rpc_call`.

throws.description: if the success `result` does not parse against `spec.output`,

declarations.name: rpc_call_typed
declarations.doc_comment:
Same as `rpc_call` but parses the success `result` through the given
output schema and returns typed data. Envelope-level failures or error
responses throw — use the untyped `rpc_call` for tests that need to
assert on specific error shapes.

throws.description: if the response is a JSON-RPC error, if `rpc_call` throws

declarations.type_signature: <T>(args: RpcCallArgs, output_schema: ZodType<T, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<T, unknown>>): Promise<T>

declarations.name: find_rpc_action
declarations.doc_comment:
Find the `RpcAction` for a method within a set of RPC endpoint specs.
Returns both the endpoint path and the matched action. `undefined` when
the method is not registered.
declarations.type_signature: (rpc_endpoints: readonly RpcEndpointSpec[], method: string): { path: string; action: RpcAction; } | undefined

declarations.name: find_rpc_method
declarations.doc_comment:
Find the generated surface entry for a method — the shape returned by
`generate_app_surface` (JSON-serializable, useful for schema assertions
at the boundary of a consumer test).
declarations.type_signature: (rpc_endpoints: readonly AppSurfaceRpcEndpoint[], method: string): { path: string; method_spec: AppSurfaceRpcMethod; } | undefined

declarations.name: require_rpc_endpoint_path
declarations.doc_comment:
Resolve a single RPC endpoint path — the common case where a consumer
mounts exactly one `create_rpc_endpoint`.

Used at suite setup time to hard-fail integration suites (admin / audit /
SSE / rate-limiting) when the consumer omitted `rpc_endpoints` rather
than letting tests fail mid-run with confusing errors.

Callers that need multi-endpoint support should iterate `rpc_endpoints`
directly.

throws.description: if `rpc_endpoints` is empty (hard-fail; see the suite options

JSON-RPC test helpers — request construction, response assertion, and
one-shot call ergonomics.

Shared by `testing/rpc_attack_surface.ts`, `testing/rpc_round_trip.ts`, and
consumer-facing admin/audit suites that exercise RPC methods directly.

RPC endpoint specs — the source `RpcAction` arrays for params generation.

Accepts either an array (eager) or a factory
`(ctx: AppServerContext) => Array<RpcEndpointSpec>` — the factory form

invoked once at setup (via a stub ctx) to enumerate methods and

declarations.name: describe_rpc_round_trip_tests
declarations.doc_comment:
Run schema-driven round-trip validation for RPC endpoints.

For each method:
1. Generate valid params from the action's input schema
2. Fire a POST request with JSON-RPC envelope
3. For `side_effects: false` methods, also fire a GET request
4. Validate response is well-formed JSON-RPC; successful responses are
   also validated against the method's declared output schema

Error responses (from missing DB state, etc.) are expected and validated
as well-formed JSON-RPC errors. Successful responses are validated against
`action.spec.output`.

modules.module_comment:
Schema-driven round-trip validation for RPC endpoints.

For every RPC method, generates valid params and fires JSON-RPC requests
(POST for all methods, GET for reads), validating that responses are
well-formed JSON-RPC. Successful responses are validated against the
method's declared output schema. DB-backed via `create_test_app`.

declarations.name: detect_format
declarations.doc_comment:
Detect format constraints on a field by converting to JSON Schema.
Returns format string (e.g. 'uuid', 'email') or null.

declarations.doc_comment: Generate a valid-ish value for a field based on its base type.

declarations.doc_comment:
Resolve a route path with valid-ish param values so params validation passes.
Used when testing input on routes that also have params.

declarations.name: generate_valid_body
declarations.doc_comment: Generate a valid request body for a route's input schema.

throws.description: if the generated body fails `input_schema.safeParse` — catches

a generated body that passes `safeParse`, or `undefined` for null /
non-object schemas

modules.module_comment:
Schema-driven value generation helpers for testing.

Walks Zod schemas to generate valid values for route params, request bodies,
and URL paths. Used by adversarial input, adversarial 404, and round-trip
validation test suites.

declarations.name: SseRouteTestSpec
declarations.doc_comment: Config for a single SSE route under test.

properties.name: path
properties.doc_comment: Full HTTP path of the SSE endpoint (e.g., `'/api/tx/subscribe'`).

properties.name: trigger
properties.doc_comment:
Fire an event matching one of the declared `event_specs` that should
reach the open stream. Called after the `: connected` comment is observed.
The triggered frame must be a JSON-serializable `{method, params}` payload.

properties.name: event_specs
properties.doc_comment:
Event specs to validate the triggered payload against. When omitted,
the payload is only asserted to be well-formed `{method, params}`.

properties.name: assert_closes_on_revoke
properties.doc_comment:
Whether to assert the stream closes after `session_revoke_all`.
Default `true`. Set `false` for endpoints that don't wire a close-on-revoke
guard (leaves a TODO to fix, rather than silently passing).

declarations.name: SseRouteTestOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `describe_sse_route_tests`.

properties.name: session_options

properties.name: create_route_specs

properties.name: app_options

properties.name: db_factories

properties.name: on_audit_event
properties.doc_comment:
Backend audit event callback — threaded to `create_test_app_server`.
Use to wire a close-on-revoke guard for consumer SSE registries
(e.g., via `create_sse_auth_guard`) so `session_revoke_all` actually
closes the tested streams.

properties.name: rpc_endpoints
properties.doc_comment:
RPC endpoint specs — required so the close-on-revoke assertion can
dispatch `account_session_revoke_all` via RPC (the former REST route
`POST /api/account/sessions/revoke-all` was removed in the 2026-04-23
migration). Hard-fails via `require_rpc_endpoint_path` on setup.

Accepts either an array (eager) or a factory
`(ctx: AppServerContext) => Array<RpcEndpointSpec>` — the factory form
is required when action handlers must close over the per-test
`ctx.app_settings` / `ctx.deps`. The factory must return the same
endpoint `path` regardless of ctx — it is invoked once at setup with
a stub ctx for path lookup and again per-test by `create_app_server`
for live dispatch.

properties.name: routes
properties.doc_comment: SSE routes to exercise.

declarations.name: describe_sse_route_tests
declarations.doc_comment:
Run SSE route validation tests.

For each route: opens an authenticated SSE connection, asserts the
connected comment, fires the trigger, validates the resulting payload,
then asserts close-on-revoke (unless opted out).

throws.description: at setup time when `options.rpc_endpoints` is empty — the

modules.module_comment:
Schema-driven SSE route validation test suite.

Complements `describe_round_trip_validation` (which skips SSE routes).
For each configured SSE route:
1. Open the stream with matching auth.
2. Assert the `: connected` comment is emitted.
3. Fire `trigger()` — expect one `data: {...}` frame.
4. Validate the payload as `{method, params}` against declared `EventSpec`s.
5. Fire `session_revoke_all` for the account and assert the stream closes
   (when `assert_closes_on_revoke !== false`).

declarations.name: StandardTestOptions

properties.name: session_options

properties.name: create_route_specs
properties.doc_comment: Route spec factory — same one used in production.

properties.name: app_options

Role schema result from `create_role_schema()`.
When provided, admin integration tests are included.

properties.name: rpc_endpoints

RPC endpoint specs — required. The standard integration tests drive

Accepts either an array (eager) or a factory
`(ctx: AppServerContext) => Array<RpcEndpointSpec>`. Round-tripped

properties.name: admin_prefix
properties.doc_comment:
Path prefix where admin routes are mounted.
Default `'/api/admin'`.

Run both standard integration and admin integration test suites.

Admin tests are only included when `roles` is provided.

modules.module_comment:
Combined standard test suite helper.

Convenience wrapper that runs both `describe_standard_integration_tests`
and `describe_standard_admin_integration_tests` in a single call.
Existing per-suite calls keep working — this is purely additive.

declarations.name: create_throwing_stub
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a Proxy that throws descriptive errors on any property access or method call.

Use for deps that should never be reached during a test. If a test accidentally
calls through to a throwing stub, the error message identifies exactly which stub
was hit, catching test bugs that would silently pass with `{} as any`.

JS-internal probes (`Symbol`, `then`, `constructor`, `$$typeof`) return
`undefined` so the proxy doesn't crash framework-level identity checks;
`toJSON` returns `"[throwing_stub:label]"` so accidental serialization
surfaces the stub's identity in console output rather than silent `"{}"`.

throws.description: on any non-internal property access, labeled with the stub

declarations.name: create_noop_stub
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a Proxy where every method access returns a no-op async function.

Use for deps that may be reached during "correct auth passes guard" tests
but whose return values don't matter. Unlike the explicit method listing,
this auto-updates when interfaces change.
declarations.type_signature: <T = any>(_label: string, overrides?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined): T

declarations.name: stub
declarations.doc_comment: Throwing stub — use for deps that should never be reached.

declarations.name: create_stub_db
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a stub `Db` for handler tests that use `apply_route_specs` with declarative transactions.

Returns a real `Db` instance with:
- `query` returns empty rows (safety net for unmocked query functions)
- `query_one` returns undefined
- `transaction(fn)` calls `fn(db)` synchronously (no real transaction)

declarations.name: stub_handler
declarations.doc_comment: Stub handler that returns a 200 response.

declarations.name: stub_mw
declarations.doc_comment: Stub middleware that passes through.

declarations.name: stub_app_deps
declarations.doc_comment: Stub `AppDeps` for auth surface tests — throws on any method access.

declarations.name: create_stub_app_deps
declarations.doc_comment: Create no-op `AppDeps` for auth surface testing.

declarations.name: create_stub_api_middleware
declarations.doc_comment: Create the API middleware stub array matching `create_auth_middleware_specs` output.

declarations.name: create_stub_app_server_context
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a stub `AppServerContext` for attack surface testing.

Provides sensible defaults for all fields. Pass `session_options` since
it varies per consumer; other fields use stubs/nulls.

parameters.name: session_options
parameters.description: consumer's session config (required — varies per app)

declarations.name: CreateTestAppSurfaceSpecOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `create_test_app_surface_spec`.

properties.name: session_options

properties.name: create_route_specs

properties.name: env_schema

properties.name: event_specs

RPC endpoint specs for surface generation.

Accepts either an array (eager) or a factory
`(ctx: AppServerContext) => Array<RpcEndpointSpec>` — symmetric with

declarations.name: create_test_app_surface_spec
declarations.doc_comment:
Create an `AppSurfaceSpec` for attack surface testing.

Mirrors `create_app_server`'s route assembly: consumer routes +
factory-managed bootstrap routes + surface generation. If
`create_app_server` changes how it wires routes, update this helper
to stay in sync (single source of truth for all consumers).

modules.module_comment:
Stub factories for auth surface testing.

Provides throwing stubs (catch unexpected access), no-op stubs (allow access
without side effects), and pre-built bundles for `AppDeps`.

declarations.name: assert_protected_routes_declare_401
declarations.doc_comment: Every protected route has 401 in `error_schemas`.

declarations.name: assert_role_routes_declare_403
declarations.doc_comment: Every role/keeper route has 403 in `error_schemas`.

declarations.name: assert_input_routes_declare_400
declarations.doc_comment: Every route with non-null `input_schema` has 400 in `error_schemas`.

declarations.name: assert_params_routes_declare_400
declarations.doc_comment: Every route with non-null `params_schema` has 400 in `error_schemas`.

declarations.name: assert_query_routes_declare_400
declarations.doc_comment: Every route with non-null `query_schema` has 400 in `error_schemas`.

declarations.name: assert_descriptions_present
declarations.doc_comment: Every route has a non-empty description.

declarations.name: assert_no_duplicate_routes
declarations.doc_comment: No duplicate method+path pairs.

declarations.name: assert_middleware_errors_propagated
declarations.doc_comment:
Every applicable middleware that declares errors must have those status codes
present in the route's `error_schemas`.

declarations.name: assert_error_schemas_structurally_valid
declarations.doc_comment:
Every route's declared error schemas must have an `error` field at the top level
(conforming to the `ApiError` base shape `{error: string}`).

Catches typos in error schema definitions and ensures consumers can always
read `.error` from error responses.

declarations.name: assert_error_code_status_consistency
declarations.doc_comment:
The same `z.literal()` error code should not appear at different HTTP status codes
across routes.

Extracts `const` values from error schema `error` properties (which correspond to
`z.literal()` in the Zod source). Flags when the same literal appears at different
status codes — e.g., `ERROR_INVALID_CREDENTIALS` at both 401 and 403 would be a bug.

Only checks schemas with `const` values (literal schemas). Generic `z.string()`
schemas (which produce `{type: 'string'}` in JSON Schema) are ignored.

declarations.name: assert_404_schemas_use_specific_errors
declarations.doc_comment:
Routes declaring 404 error schemas should use specific `z.literal()` or `z.enum()`
error codes, not generic `z.string()`.

A generic 404 schema (`ApiError` with `z.string()`) means the error code is
unconstrained — the handler could return any string, making client error handling
fragile. Routes with params (`:id`) are the primary 404 producers; their error
schemas should use specific constants like `ERROR_ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND`.

Only flags routes that have `params_schema` (param-driven resource lookup) — routes
declaring 404 for other reasons (e.g., bootstrap not configured) may legitimately
use generic schemas.

declarations.name: ErrorSchemaSpecificity
declarations.doc_comment: Specificity level of an error schema's `error` field.

declarations.name: ErrorSchemaAuditEntry
declarations.doc_comment: A single entry in the error schema tightness audit report.

properties.name: error_codes
properties.doc_comment: The literal value or enum values, if specific.

declarations.name: audit_error_schema_tightness
declarations.doc_comment:
Audit error schema tightness across all routes in a surface.

Reports which route x status code combinations use generic `ApiError`
(`z.string()`) vs specific `z.literal()` or `z.enum()` error codes.
Use the output to prioritize progressive tightening of error schemas.
declarations.return_description: audit entries for every route x status combination

declarations.name: SurfaceSecurityPolicyOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Configuration for security policy invariants.

All fields have sensible defaults. Pass overrides for project-specific needs.

properties.name: sensitive_route_patterns
properties.doc_comment:
Path patterns for routes that should be rate-limited.
Default: common sensitive REST patterns (login, password, bootstrap).
`account_token_create` became RPC-only in the 2026-04-23 migration;
per-method RPC rate limiting is a separate invariant if consumers want it.

properties.name: public_mutation_allowlist
properties.doc_comment:
Routes explicitly allowed to be public mutations (e.g., webhooks, bootstrap).
Format: `'METHOD /path'` (e.g., `'POST /api/account/login'`).

properties.name: keeper_route_prefixes
properties.doc_comment:
Allowed path prefixes for keeper-protected routes.
Default: `['/api/']`. Catches keeper routes outside expected namespaces.

declarations.name: assert_sensitive_routes_rate_limited
declarations.doc_comment:
Sensitive routes must declare rate limiting (`rate_limit_key` is non-null)
or have 429 in their error schemas.

Matches routes against sensitive patterns and flags any that lack rate limit
declarations. Catches forgotten rate limiting on credential-handling routes.

declarations.name: assert_no_unexpected_public_mutations
declarations.doc_comment:
Public mutation routes (auth: none + is_mutation) must be in the allowlist.

Catches accidentally unprotected POST/PUT/DELETE routes. Routes like login
and bootstrap are public mutations by design — they go in the allowlist.

declarations.name: assert_mutation_routes_use_post
declarations.doc_comment:
Routes with non-null input schemas should use POST (or other mutation methods),
not GET.

GET routes with request bodies are technically allowed by HTTP but semantically
suspicious — they bypass browser security assumptions about GET being idempotent.
Query-string-driven filtering (audit log, list endpoints) should use params schemas
or query string parsing, not input schemas.

Note: RPC endpoints (`create_rpc_endpoint`) use `input: z.null()` on their
route specs — the dispatcher handles body/query parsing internally. Real input
schemas live in `rpc_endpoints` surface, not on routes.

declarations.name: assert_keeper_routes_under_prefix
declarations.doc_comment:
Keeper-protected routes must be under expected path prefixes.

Catches keeper routes accidentally placed outside the API namespace
(e.g., a keeper route at `/health` or `/admin/` instead of `/api/...`).

declarations.name: ErrorSchemaTightnessOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `assert_error_schema_tightness`.

properties.name: min_specificity
properties.doc_comment: Minimum specificity level. Error schemas below this threshold fail. Default: `'enum'`.

properties.name: ignore_statuses
properties.doc_comment: HTTP status codes to skip (e.g., middleware-injected codes).

properties.name: allowlist
properties.doc_comment: Routes to skip, in `'METHOD /path'` format.

declarations.name: FUZ_APP_STOCK_ROUTE_TIGHTNESS_ALLOWLIST
declarations.doc_comment:
Routes shipped by fuz_app whose error schemas require a tightness exemption.

Currently empty — every fuz_app-shipped route (account login/password/
bootstrap/signup, db health/tables/:name/tables/:name/rows/:id) was tightened
in place to `z.enum([...])` / `z.literal(...)` against every emit-site error
code.

Kept as a forward-compatibility hook: when new stock routes ship with
heterogeneous error surfaces that need an interim generic schema, add
them here instead of forcing every consumer to hand-maintain the entry.

Paths assume the standard `/api/account` + `/api/db` prefixes used by every
fuz_app consumer. Merged into `DEFAULT_ERROR_SCHEMA_TIGHTNESS.allowlist` so
consumers calling `assert_error_schema_tightness` directly inherit the
exemptions; the standard attack-surface suite also prepends these entries
underneath any consumer-supplied allowlist so project-specific entries are
additive.

declarations.name: DEFAULT_ERROR_SCHEMA_TIGHTNESS
declarations.doc_comment:
Baseline error schema tightness applied by
`describe_standard_attack_surface_tests` when no config is passed.

Uses `min_specificity: 'enum'` (the assertion default) with `ignore_statuses`
for middleware-derived status codes that are commonly generic (auth middleware
produces multiple error codes at 401/403, and 429 comes from rate limiters),
and `allowlist` seeded with `FUZ_APP_STOCK_ROUTE_TIGHTNESS_ALLOWLIST` so
fuz_app-shipped routes with heterogeneous generic schemas don't force every
consumer to hand-maintain an identical allowlist. Consumers can pass a
narrower config with project-specific `allowlist` entries, or pass `null`
to skip the assertion entirely.

declarations.name: assert_error_schema_tightness
declarations.doc_comment:
Assert that all error schemas meet a minimum specificity threshold.

Calls `audit_error_schema_tightness` and fails on any entry below
the configured threshold. Use `allowlist` and `ignore_statuses` to exclude
known exceptions during progressive tightening.

throws.description: listing every route × status combination whose error

declarations.name: assert_surface_invariants
declarations.doc_comment:
Run all structural invariants. Options-free — applies universally.

Catches schema/surface generation bugs: missing 401/403/400 declarations,
empty descriptions, duplicate routes, middleware-injected error codes
unpropagated to routes, structurally invalid error schemas, error codes
appearing at multiple statuses, and generic 404 schemas on param routes.

throws.description: on the first invariant violation; the message names

declarations.name: assert_surface_security_policy
declarations.doc_comment:
Run security policy invariants. Configurable with sensible defaults.

Checks:
- Sensitive routes are rate-limited
- No unexpected public mutation routes
- Input schemas use mutation methods (not GET)
- Keeper routes under expected prefixes

throws.description: on the first policy violation; the message names

modules.module_comment:
Surface invariant assertions for `AppSurface` data.

Two categories:
- **Structural** — validate internal consistency (error schema presence,
  descriptions, duplicates, middleware propagation, error schema validity,
  error code consistency). No options needed.
- **Policy** — enforce security policy (sensitive routes rate-limited,
  no public mutations, mutation method conventions). Configurable with
  sensible defaults.

Structural invariants catch schema/surface generation bugs. Policy invariants
catch security misconfigurations. Both propagate automatically to consumers
via `assert_surface_invariants`.

declarations.name: FakeWs
declarations.doc_comment:
A `WSContext` paired with capture arrays. Use `sends` to assert on
outgoing frames; use `closes` to assert on revocation / close.

declarations.name: create_fake_ws
declarations.doc_comment:
Build a real `WSContext` backed by in-memory `send`/`close` capture.
Parsing of outgoing frames is left to the caller — `sends` holds the
raw strings as the dispatcher wrote them.

declarations.name: FakeHonoContextOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `create_fake_hono_context`.

properties.name: role
properties.doc_comment: A single role to grant via `create_test_request_context`.

properties.name: request_context
properties.doc_comment:
Override the `RequestContext` outright (for multi-role or custom
account/actor fixtures). Takes precedence over `role`.

declarations.name: create_fake_hono_context
declarations.doc_comment:
Build a fake Hono `Context` exposing the auth keys the dispatcher
reads via `c.get(...)`. Only `.get()` is populated — no other Hono
context surface is simulated.

declarations.name: StubUpgrade
declarations.doc_comment: The return of `create_stub_upgrade` — fake `upgradeWebSocket` + factory capture.

declarations.name: create_stub_upgrade
declarations.doc_comment:
Build a fake `upgradeWebSocket` that captures the `createEvents`
callback. The returned middleware is inert — tests drive
`createEvents` directly.

declarations.name: MinimalActionEnvironment
declarations.doc_comment:
Minimal `ActionEventEnvironment` for tests that instantiate an
`ActionPeer` without pulling in the full runtime. Pre-loads a
spec map from the supplied list.

members.type_signature: (specs: readonly ({ method: string; initiator: "frontend" | "backend" | "both"; side_effects: boolean; input: ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>; output: ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>; ... 6 more ...; rate_limit?: "both" | ... 2 more ... | undefined; } | { ...; } | { ...; })[]): MinimalActionEnvironment
